# Meeting Summary for AI WG

- Meeting start: 2026-03-26T17:27:36Z
- Meeting end: 2026-03-26T18:06:55Z
- Summary start: 2026-03-26T17:27:37Z
- Summary end: 2026-03-26T18:04:29Z

The AI Working Group meeting focused on several key updates and discussions. Martin presented a new tagline proposal for GraphQL.org, suggesting "API language for humans and agents" as an update to the current description. Mark shared progress on a mock directive specification for GraphQL, which allows developers to mock API responses locally during development. Jose provided updates on natural language to GraphQL query work, including discussions about embedding models and benchmarking approaches, as well as their implementation of traffic-based field scoring improvements. The group also discussed the need for a reference implementation and guidance document for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems.

## Next Steps

- Martin: Launch the call for projects for AI-related (and other) grants in the coming days, including updating the list of projects in the pull request and inviting community members to add project ideas or file grant proposals.
- All interested parties: Add project ideas to the call for projects pull request if there are areas where help is needed.
- All interested parties: Provide feedback, comments, approvals, or thumbs up on the proposed GraphQL.org tagline change in the relevant GitHub issue.
- Martin: Update all instances of the GraphQL.org tagline, including the header where it says "a query language for your API," as part of the pull request(s).
- Martin: Bring the proposed tagline update to the TSE meetings and, if needed, to the board for final approval.
- Mark: Incorporate pending feedback on the mock directive spec (GAP 10) and keep the discussion open regarding fragment-level and nested mocks, updating the spec as needed.
- Jose/Kewei/Thor/Team: Continue benchmarking the use of Quen (QN) vs Dragon Roberta for embedding generation, and share results/learnings with the group.
- Jose/Kewei/Thor/Team: Work towards creating a blog post or lightweight reference/recommendation on best practices, metrics, and optimizations for RAG systems, including guidance on improving RAG quality.
- Kewei/Team: Keep the group posted on further experiments and improvements to RAG systems, including the use of traffic-based ranking and path/fragment embeddings.

## Summary

### AI and Cloud Tools Discussion

The meeting began with Martin and Mark discussing an AI personality experiment at work and a new Cloud dashboard tool. They examined language usage data, noting TypeScript as the top language and discussing the absence of GraphQL in the data. Kewei then took over as host, introducing attendees and explaining the meeting's purpose and guidelines, including the use of AI for summarization and note-taking. The meeting was in progress with introductions ongoing when the transcript ended.

### Grants and Website Updates Discussion

The team discussed two main topics: grants and website updates. Martin announced a new grant program offering $5K grants for AI-related projects, with a call for projects to be launched soon. The team also reviewed a proposed new tagline for GraphQL.org, changing from "a query language for modern APIs" to "the API language for humans and agents." While the team liked the new tagline, they identified that additional updates might be needed in other sections of the website and agreed to gather feedback before presenting it to the board for final approval.

### GraphQL Mock Directive Specification

Mark presented a GraphQL mock directive specification being tracked in GAP-10, designed to allow developers to add mock data for both simple scalar values and complex selection sets during local development. The specification currently supports operation-level and field-level mocking but not nested mocks, as this would create ambiguity about which mock data takes precedence. The team discussed potential future extensions including support for inline fragments and fragment spreads, with Mark emphasizing that the current specification is a draft proposal open to feedback and modifications.

### RAG System Progress and Recommendations

The team discussed progress on embedding generation, with Thor suggesting the use of Quen (QN) instead of the current Dragon Roberta model, and Kewei confirming contact with the data team about this potential change. Kewei also shared their approach of incorporating traffic data to adjust field rankings beyond text similarity, which has shown promising results. The group agreed on creating a reference blog post outlining recommendations and best practices for RAG systems, including metrics and optimization suggestions, as a lightweight alternative to a formal specification.
